---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}

# Camille Wixon

PhD Candidate in Economics  
University of Virginia

Health Economics · Industrial Organization · Public Economics

[caw2va@virginia.edu](mailto:caw2va@virginia.edu) · [CV](files/Camille_Wixon_CV.pdf){target="_blank"}

I am a PhD candidate in Economics at the University of Virginia. My research is in health economics and industrial organization, with a focus on reproductive healthcare markets.

I am on the academic job market for 2025–2026.

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}

![](profile.jpg)

:::

:::

## Job Market Paper

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

**[Placeholder Title for Your JMP](files/wixon_jmp.pdf)**

*Abstract goes here. This is placeholder text so we can see how the layout works. When you have a real abstract, this is where it goes — probably 4–6 sentences explaining the question, the data, the method, and the headline finding. The text wraps naturally next to the figure.*

[PDF](files/wixon_jmp.pdf) · [Slides](#)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

![](profile.jpg)

:::

:::

## Working Papers

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-7}

**[Placeholder Title for IUD Duration Paper](#)**

*Another placeholder abstract. Eventually this will describe your work on strategic competition in IUD markets.*

[PDF](#) · [Slides](#)

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}

![](profile.jpg)

:::

:::

## Works in Progress

**Menopause and labor market outcomes**

**Ammunition stockpiling and gun regulation** (with Marcella Cartledge and Yiren Ding)
